newsItems.title: The European Union Satellite Center Signs ‘CISE’ Agreement to Strengthen Europe’s Maritime Security
newsItems.description: The European Union Satellite Center has signed the CISE Cooperation Agreement, establishing the framework for operational information exchange between CISE and MARSUR, enhancing Maritime Domain Awareness for the EU, SatCen, and Member States.

newsItems.title: Isar Aerospace to Launch Satellite for Planet Germany
newsItems.description: The German-based subsidiary of Planet Labs has selected Isar Aerospace to launch one of the company’s Pelican Earth observation satellites aboard a Spectrum rocket. newsItems.title: Iran was the most successful failure in US airpower history
newsItems.description: The US entered the conflict with a detailed plan for a war of destruction, but no serious plan for Iran’s war of disruption.

newsItems.title: Hydrosat Launches ‘Discovery Portal’, A Centralized Gateway to Unlock 500M sq. km. of Thermal Satellite Imagery
newsItems.description: Hydrosat has launched its Discovery Portal, a centralized gateway to its thermal satellite imagery and analytics, as it builds out its next-generation thermal imaging constellation.

newsItems.title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
newsItems.description:
Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow, the AI-powered geospatial intelligence analysis company, to build AI applications on top of Satellogic’s growing stock of satellite imagery.

newsItems.title: Rocket Lab Completes 10th Consecutive Successful ‘StriX’ Satellite Launch for Synspective
newsItems.description: Rocket Lab has completed its 10th consecutive StriX launch for Synspective, whose Earth observation constellation now numbers ten operational satellites, all deployed by Electron.

newsItems.description: NASA has delivered the first braking engines for the European Space Agency’s (ESA) Rosalind Franklin mission to Mars.
